title: Molecular genetic mechanism analysis and pedigree investigation of rare B<sub>weak</sub> subgroup.
author_string: Yuan, Xiaohua, Cong, Hui, Sun, Xiaoye
journal_full: Transfusion and apheresis science : official journal of the World Apheresis Association : official journal of the European Society for Haemapheresis
abstract: To analyze the molecular mechanism of rare B<sub>weak</sub> subgroup in the ABO blood group system and conduct pedigree investigations. The blood group was detected by conventional serological method, and ABO gene of proband and her family was amplified and sequenced by polymerase chain reaction method. The study showed that the proband was a B<sub>weak</sub> phenotype by conventional serological method. Her family's serological results were as follows, her father and eldest brother were B<sub>weak</sub> subgroup while her mother and second eldest brother were O group. The proband's ABO gene sequencing result was ABO*BW.27/ABO*O.01.02. Her father, mother and two elder brothers were ABO*BW.27/ABO*O.01.01, ABO*O.01.01/ABO*O.01.02, ABO*BW.27/ABO*O.01.02, ABO*O.01.01/ABO*O.01.02. Conventional blood group serology combined with molecular diagnostic technology can accurately identify the B<sub>weak</sub> subgroup, and the pedigree investigation analysis showed that the proband's allelic mutation came from her father. She has gained a point mutation of c.905A>G on the basis of ABO*B.01.
